reasoning: The question asks whether the NFL Media unit will be under Disney's control by the end of 2025. Search results indicate that while a major non-binding agreement was reached in August 2025 for Disney's ESPN to acquire the NFL Media assets (including NFL Network and RedZone), the transaction required regulatory review. The acquisition was officially approved by federal regulators and the deal closed on January 31, 2026. The actual transition of operations is scheduled for April 2026. In the context of M&A, an acquirer legally cannot assume "control" of the target (due to gun-jumping regulations) prior to regulatory clearance and closing. Since the deal did not close until January 2026, Disney did not have control of the NFL Media unit by the end of 2025. The high market probability in October 2025 (87%) likely reflects the market's expectation that the deal would successfully close soon following the August announcement, but regulatory review pushed the closing date just past the 2025 deadline. Therefore, it is virtually certain that it resolves NO.
